summary: Check out Botanical Bazaar festival here: вҒ https://bit.ly/3RsvdflвҒ The Gold Coast's Premier Gardening and Sustainable living festival August 2nd and 3rd.  We live, work, and dream on Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander lands. We acknowledge them as holding deep ecological knowledge and their ability to live sustainably in harmony with the land. They and other ancient cultures are roots of permacultureвҖ”working with nature, fostering resilience, and ensuring balance for future generations. Show notes: In this episode of The Practical Permaculture Podcast, Alysse sits down with the inspiring duo Ashleigh and Jarrod, who left the city, lived in a tent (WHILE PREGNANT!) to build a cozy, eco-consciousВ  built with hempcrete! (Spoiler alert вҖ”В  itвҖҷs amazing.) Join us for a chat about their journey through the highs and lows of building a sustainable home from the ground up. Navigating building codes and pushing for their non negotiable items (double glazing) Ashleigh and Jarrod share how they turned their permaculture principles into practical, livable reality вҖ” one step at a time. Expect real talk about:рҹҢұ Why they ditched the conventional path for a greener oneрҹҸ—пёҸ The world of hempcrete and climate-smart designрҹҡҪ What it really takes to manage waste sustainablyрҹҺ“ Their passion for teaching others through tours and workshops Whether you're dreaming of a sustainable build or just love a good back-to-the-land story, this episode will leave you fired up and full of ideas for building a home вҖ” and a life вҖ” that works with nature, not against it. title: Regenerative Farming: The Truth About Soil, Livestock & Sustainability
summary: In this eye-opening episode, we sit down with the team behind Mulroy Meadow Farm, pioneers in regenerative agriculture. Discover their journey from conventional farming methods to embracing natureвҖҷs own systems вҖ” blending science, tradition, and passion to restore soil health and nurture biodiversity.We tackle common misconceptions about meat, chemicals, and farming practices, and why livestock management is at the heart of true sustainability. Plus, hear firsthand about the challenges they face, from soil compaction and predators to the patience needed for real change.Whether youвҖҷre a farmer, a food lover, or just curious about the future of agriculture, this episode offers a fresh perspective on how farming can be part of the solution вҖ” not the problem.Follow Mulroy Meadow Farm:Instagram & Facebook: @mulroymeadowfarm

title: From Conflict to Connection: Transforming Relationships Through the Enneagram with Christa Hardin, MA
summary: In this episode of Typology, we take a look at the fascinating intersection of the Enneagram and relationships with our special guest, Crista Hardin, a relationship expert and author of "The Enneagram in Marriage." Christa, an Enneagram 7, brings her vibrant energy and insights to the conversation, discussing how her type influences her marriage and her work with couples.  We explore the complexities of anxiety in Sevens, the importance of understanding core fears in relationships, and how different Enneagram types interact with one another. Christa shares her personal experiences, including her journey through marriage and the challenges she and her husband have faced over nearly 30 years together.  Throughout the episode, we touch on the dynamics of various type pairings, the challenges and transformations that can arise in relationships, and the significance of emotional regulation and understanding in conflict resolution. Christa emphasizes the importance of recognizing our own flaws and the value of shared meaning in relationships.  Join us for this enlightening discussion that not only sheds light on the Enneagram but also offers practical insights for navigating the complexities of love and partnership. Don't forget to check out Christa's podcast, "Enneagram and Marriage," and her website for more resources!  You can find Christa's website, blog, freebies, and Enneagram & Marriage podcast links atВ www.enneagramandmarriage.com. title: Backyard Burnouts to Blowing Up at SEMA Westin Champlin
summary: YouвҖҷve seen the SmokeStang light up burnouts on YouTubeвҖ”now hear the backstory straight from Westen Champlin himself. WeвҖҷre talking about how a $1500 junkyard 12-valve turned into a 1200+ horsepower weapon, what really goes down behind the scenes, and why building fast diesel rigs usually ends with something on fire (or broken). From transmission failures and sketchy last-minute wiring to full-send dyno runs and burnout comps, this episode covers the kind of chaos that comes with chasing big power on tight timelines. Westen shares what itвҖҷs like growing a YouTube channel while juggling builds, towing rigs across the country, and trying to keep it all from blowing apart. We get into the crossover between diesel drag racing, truck pulling, and burnout cultureвҖ”and how U.S. events are finally catching up to the Aussies when it comes to putting on a real show. WeвҖҷre also talking about the upcoming Horsepower Rodeo going down May 24th in Winfield, KansasвҖ”30+ pro-level builds, blown methanol setups, diesel trucks, and zero lift off the throttle. If youвҖҷre into diesel performance, race builds, YouTube truck content, or just want to hear what happens when you stop caring about rules and start chasing horsepower, this oneвҖҷs worth your time.

title: Kohji Nagata вҖ” Building Parallel, Designing with Obsession, and Falling in Love with Your Creations (#84)
summary: Kohji Nagata has worn many hatsвҖ”software developer, musician, podcast creatorвҖ”but today heвҖҷs best known as Head of Design and co-founder of Parallel Studios, where he helped launch the massively successful Web3 trading card game Parallel. What started as a passion project has become a genre-defining phenomenon, valued at one point at half a billion dollars. Kohji approaches design from a deep philosophical place, wrestling with fear of failure, perfectionism, and the drive to always improve. In this episode, he and Justin explore the creative tension between success and self-doubt, dissect the mechanics of resource systems in TCGs, and dig into the role of AI in the future of game design. Whether you're an aspiring creator or a veteran designer, this conversation offers a rare window into the mind of someone building at the frontier of games and technology.   This is a public episode. title: #29 125er-Champs 2025 вҖ“ Event und Bikes
summary: Bereits zum vierten Mal fighten 2025 neun nigelnagelneue 125er-Bikes um den Titel 125er-Champ des Jahres. Und wie immer zГӨhlt dabei allein das Votum der jugendlichen Teilnehmer*innen. Welche Bikes in welchen Gruppen dabei sind? Wir verraten es euch.  An den Zutaten fГјr die 125er-Champs 2025 ГӨndern wir nichts. Wie immer stehen acht unterschiedliche 125er-Modelle in vier verschiedenen Gruppen bereit, um unter sich das beste 125er-Modell einer Kategorie auszumachen. Und diese vier Sieger treffen dann im Finale der 125er-Champs auf den Regenten des letzten Jahres вҖ“ die KTM 125er Duke. Wer weiterkommt, wer die einzelnen Gruppensiege einfГӨhrt, welches Bike im Finale wann gehen muss вҖ“ all das bestimmen wieder fГјnf jugendliche Gasttester*innen, die mit uns zwei Tage Гјber die schwГӨbische Alb dГјsen. Die dГјrfen sich Stand Juni 2025 auf folgende Kategorien freuen: Naked Bike, Supermoto, Cruiser und Sportler. Da sind dann heiГҹe Eisen wie Fantics neue nackte Rakete Stealth 125 dabei, aber auch KTMs hochbeiniger Sumo-Flitzer 125 SMC R. Wer es eher gemГјtlich mag, fГӨhrt vielleicht auf die neue Hyosung GV 125i ABS ab. Oder doch lieber sportlich die Kurve kratzen: Dann wГӨre die R 125 von Yamaha genau richtig. Auf all diese Modelle wartet im Finale die KTM 125 Duke, die ihren Titel aus dem letzten Jahr verteidigen will. Klingt alles ultraspannend? Dann jetzt reingehГ¶rt. Bewerben kГ¶nnt ihr euch noch bis zum 23. Juni 2025 fГјr die Testfahrten zu den 125er-Champs 2025. Was ihr dafГјr mitbringen mГјsst? Auch das verraten wir euch in dieser Podcast-Folge.

title: Why Ponder is Losing Its Edge in Legacy
summary: Zac and Phil discuss the declining effectiveness of the card Ponder in the current Magic: The Gathering meta. They explore how Ponder is too slow for many decks, particularly in a fast-paced environment filled with powerful threats. The discussion highlights the importance of card density and the need for more effective answers in control decks. The impact of the London Mulligan on deck-building strategies is also examined, emphasizing the shift in how players approach their card choices in response to evolving gameplay dynamics. In this conversation, the hosts delve into the evolving role of the card Ponder in competitive Magic: The Gathering gameplay. They discuss how the current game dynamics have shifted, making Ponder less effective and emphasizing the need for more impactful threats in decks. The discussion also covers the importance of decision-making in gameplay, particularly in recognizing when an opponent is making suboptimal plays. The conversation concludes with reflections on the future of cantrips in competitive play, suggesting a shift away from traditional strategies.

title: Moths, Meadows and Mischievous Mutts
summary: Biodiverse spaces are important not only for healthy ecosystems but they also bring tangible benefits for our mental wellness as well. Unfortunately new data shows that some of our most loved garden visitors вҖ“ the Lepidoptera, or butterflies and moths вҖ“ are in alarming decline. WeвҖҷll be finding out from Dr Richard Fox, Head of Science at Butterfly Conservation, how gardeners can give them the boost they need. Ed Cooper from the trials team down at RHS Garden Wisley shows us another way of welcoming in not only butterflies and moths, but a whole host of other wildlife as well, with advice on sowing annual and perennial wildflower meadows. And finally, we find out how to maximise our outdoor spaces for our four legged friends, with Lead Horticulturist Dawn Grehan, at Battersea Dogs and Cats Home. В  Host:В Gareth Richards В  Contributors:В Dr Richard Fox, Ed Cooper, Dawn Grehan Links:В  Butterfly Conservation Butterfly recording and monitoring RHS Plants for Pollinators RHS Chelsea Flower Show How to create a pet-friendly garden Potentially harmful garden plants

title: How A Multi-Tiered Governance Drives Healthcare Supply Chain Success with Conrad Emmerich, Advocate Health's Chief Procurement Officer and SVP of Supply Chain Management
summary: A robust governance structure, strong clinical relationships, and a focus on people are crucial for achieving supply chain excellence in healthcare.В  In this episode, Conrad Emmerich, Chief Procurement Officer and Senior Vice President of Supply Chain Management at Advocate Health, shares how his organization consistently earns recognition from Gartner for supply chain excellence. He explains that their clinically driven decision-making is supported by a robust governance structure involving over 450 team members across all levels. Conrad emphasizes the importance of developing and empowering their people and highlights their digital transformation, including a unified ERP system and advanced analytics that were crucial during Hurricane Helene. He concludes by stressing the growing need for supply chain leaders to be strategic, expert contributors within their organizations. Tune in and learn how these strategies improve patient outcomes, manage costs, and drive innovation! Resources:    Connect with and follow Conrad Emmerich on LinkedIn.   Follow Advocate Health on LinkedIn and explore their website.

title: Cold exposure, saunas and your health: what the science says | Dr. Susanna SГёberg & Prof. Tim Spector
summary: Unwrap the truth about your food рҹ‘ү вҒ Get ZOEвҖҷs new app  Most of us avoid the cold. We crank up the heating, bundle up in layers, and curse every icy gust of wind. But what if freezing вҖ” or even sweating вҖ” could be the key to better health? In this episode, Jonathan is joined by two world-leading scientists to explore a radical idea: that extreme temperatures might unlock powerful benefits for your metabolism, mental health, and even longevity. Dr. Susanna SГёberg, the researcher who coined the вҖңSГёberg Principle,вҖқ has spent years studying the effects of cold plunges and saunas on the human body. Her findings? Just minutes of exposure a few times a week could improve insulin sensitivity, activate brown fat, and lower stress. SheвҖҷs joined by Prof. Tim Spector - professor of genetic epidemiology at KingвҖҷs College London, one of the worldвҖҷs top 100 most cited scientists, and scientific co-founder at ZOE - who explains how these temperature shocks may even impact your gut microbiome. title: Michelle Money Weir on Reinvention & Life After The Bachelor

title: Biennials to sow from seed to fill the May gap - Episode 224
podcast_name: grow, cook, eat, arrange with Sarah Raven & friends

title: #38 Re-thinking Exercise:  New Evidence, Smarter Moves
podcast_name: Live Long and Well with Dr. Bobby
summary: Send us a text Take the Health Type Quiz or Join the Newsletter here Please give me podcast feedback with a few questions here In this episode, I revisit the most powerful tool for longevityвҖ”exerciseвҖ”and unpack fresh research that changes how we should think about its timing, type, and impact. We begin with a quick recap of the foundational elements covered in Episode 2, emphasizing that aerobic activity can lower the risk of mortality, heart attack, stroke, cancer, and dementia by 20вҖ“30%, even when started later in life.В  Then we explore compelling new studies that show how exercise affects everything from blood pressure to brain volume. A meta-analysis of 14,000 participants shows even 15вҖ“20 minutes of moderate exercise daily lowers blood pressure. A review of 19 trials links physical activity to better sleep efficiency and reduced wake time. In terms of weight management, a meta-analysis of 116 randomized trials finds that even 30 minutes of exercise weekly leads to measurable weight loss, with a dose-response up to 300 minutes per week. Exercise also appears to influence mood. A review of 33 observational studies involving nearly 100,000 adults suggests that 5,000вҖ“7,000 steps daily correlate with reduced depressive symptoms. While causality remains uncertain, the association is intriguing. Beyond mental health, strength training may reduce inflammation: 19 trials show CRP levels dropped inВ  adults who engaged in resistance training. Perhaps most excitingly, a smaller study found higher cardiorespiratory fitness linked to greater hippocampal volume and better memory in older adults. To optimize these benefits, we explore emerging science around exercise timing and routine structure. A large study of 14,000 users wearing devices found that intense workouts within two hours of bedtime delayed sleep onset by 36 minutes. Perhaps finish workouts at least four hours before sleep for better rest. Interestingly, data from over 400,000 adults reveals that women gain more longevity benefit from exercise than menвҖ”and with less time invested. We also cover weekend warriors. A UK Biobank study showed no difference in health outcomes based on when exercise occurred, debunking the belief that daily consistency is essentialвҖ”what matters is that you do it. If you're sleep-deprived, caffeine might restore performance, as shown in a 10K time trial study. Lastly, a novel study using post-workout cold exposure showed that cold immersion reduced blood flow and amino acid uptake for up to 3 hours.

summary: Imagine turning construction waste into functional products like stepping stones and garbage cans. What about printing a custom home retrofit piece in hours instead of weeks? They also talk about the benefits of using 3D printing in construction and how 3D printing is considered a green building technology. 3D printing offers a sustainable solution to not only recycle but also regenerate this plastic waste into durable, functional products for the construction industry and beyond. title: Ep 156 вҖ“ Carpal tunnel syndrome
summary: Carpal tunnel syndrome (CTS) is the most common entrapment neuropathy, primarily affecting the median nerve at the level of the wrist. The condition results from compression of the median nerve within the carpal tunnel, leading to sensory and motor impairments in the affected hand. There are a range of causes for the condition, and treatments range from simple conservative measures to surgery. In this episode, Dr Roger Henderson looks at how to recognise the condition, some of the clinical and diagnostic tests available, when to refer to secondary care and management options including both surgical and non-surgical measures. Access episode show notes containing key references and take-home points at: https://gpnotebook.com/en-GB/podcasts/musculoskeletal-medicine/ep-156-carpal-tunnel-syndrome. Did you know? жң¬жңҹгҖҠдҪ пјҢйқҷдёҚдёӢжқҘгҖӢйқһеёёжңүе№ёжқҘеҲ°дёҠжө·пјҢеҠ е…ҘеҲ°вҖңдёүеҘҪе§җеҰ№вҖқе–ңеү§е‘Ёд№ӢдёӯпјҢйқҷе§җдёҺ5дҪҚдјҳз§Җзҡ„и„ұеҸЈз§ҖеҘіжј”е‘ҳдёҖиө·и·ҹеӨ§е®¶иҒҠиҒҠйӮЈдәӣеҝ«д№җиҖҢдёҚеҶ…еҚ·зҡ„жҲҗй•ҝж•…дәӢпјҢи®©еӨ§е®¶зңӢзңӢеҘіз”ҹе№Ҫй»ҳиө·жқҘиғҪжҺҖзҝ»еҮ еқ—еӨ©иҠұжқҝпјҒ еӨ§е®¶д»ҺвҖңи°ҒеңЁе®ҡд№үеҘіжҖ§зҡ„дҝқиҙЁжңҹвҖқејҖе§ӢиҒҠиө·пјҢйқҷе§җеҲҶдә«дәҶе№ҙиҪ»ж—¶зҡ„йӣ„еҝғе’Ңжӣҙе№ҙжңҹзҡ„иҮӘжҲ‘жҖҖз–‘пјӣеҳ»е“ҲвҖңиҒ’еҷӘвҖқзҡ„еҲҶдә«з©әдёӯи¶ЈдәӢпјӣдёүејҹеңЁзҺ°еңәе®үйқҷзҡ„вҖңеҒ·жҮ’вҖқпјҢж·Ўж·Ўең°жҗһз¬‘пјӣйўңжҖЎгҖҒйўңжӮҰеЁ“еЁ“и®Іиҝ°з”ҹжҙ»йҮҢзҡ„и§ӮеҜҹе’ҢеҘҮжҖқеҰҷжғіпјӣжӯҘе§җдёӯж°”еҚҒи¶ізҡ„йј“еҠұжҜҸдёӘеҘіеӯ©йғҪиҰҒзҲұиҮӘе·ұгҖӮ еңЁеҜ№и°ҲдёӯпјҢеӨ§е®¶жҠӣејҖдёҖеҲҮдё»д№үпјҢжү“з ҙдәҶе№ҙйҫ„зҡ„еҲҶз•ҢпјҢдёәжҲ‘д»¬еұ•зӨәдәҶеӨҡж ·зҡ„еҘіжҖ§ж ·иІҢгҖӮйҮҺеҝғпјҢдҫ ж°”пјҢеҝ«ж„ҸпјҢеҶ·йқҷпјҢжҒ¬ж·ЎпјҢжҡ—ж¶ҢпјҢжІ®дё§пјҢз»Ҫж”ҫпјҢжҜҸдёҖдҪҚеҘіжҖ§пјҢйғҪеҖјеҫ—жӣҙдё°еҜҢзҡ„иҜҚжұҮеҺ»еҪўе®№пјҢиҖҢйқһе®ҡд№үгҖӮ иҝҷеңәеҜ№и°ҲпјҢж— и®әжҳҜиҒ’еҷӘзқҖжҗһз¬‘пјҢиҝҳжҳҜе®үйқҷзқҖиЎЁиҫҫпјҢйғҪеңЁеҠӘеҠӣжү“з ҙйӮЈйҒ“еҶҷж»Ўе®ҡд№үзҡ„еўҷеЈҒгҖӮж¬ўиҝҺеҗ¬еҲ°иҝҷжңҹж’ӯе®ўзҡ„дҪ пјҢеңЁиҜ„и®әеҢәеҲҶдә«дҪ зҡ„жҲҗй•ҝж•…дәӢгҖӮи®©жҲ‘д»¬д»ҺвҖңзңӢдёҚи§ҒвҖқеҲ°вҖңзңӢи§ҒвҖқпјҢдёҖиө·и·ҹйҡҸиҝҷдёӘж—¶д»ЈпјҢе–ңж¬ўеҘіжҖ§зҡ„жҜҸдёҖеІҒпјҢеҲӣж–°иҮӘе·ұзҡ„жҜҸдёҖеІҒгҖӮ гҖҗдё»ж’ӯгҖ‘ жқҺйқҷ гҖҗеҳүе®ҫгҖ‘ дёүејҹ/еҳ»е“Ҳ/жӯҘжғҠдә‘/йўңжҖЎ/йўңжӮҰ гҖҗж—¶й—ҙиҪҙгҖ‘ 0пјҡ57 В В дёүеҘҪе§җеҰ№е‘ЁжҲ‘д»¬жқҘдәҶпјҒ 1пјҡ55 В жӯЈзҜҮејҖе§Ӣ 3пјҡ03 В 30+40+50+пјҢи°ҒеңЁе®ҡд№үеҘідәәзҡ„дҝқйІңжңҹпјҹ 5пјҡ42 В жӯҘжғҠдә‘пјҡжҲ‘дёҖзӣҙд»ҘдёәвҖңжӣҙе№ҙжңҹвҖқжҳҜдёӘиҙ¬д№үиҜҚ 7пјҡ35 В еҳ»е“Ҳпјҡжӣҙе№ҙжңҹзҡ„еҗҢдәӢеңЁиҒҢеңәйқўдёҙз”ҹзҗҶе’Ңжғ…з»ӘжҢ‘жҲҳ 9пјҡ28 В з§‘жҷ®пјҡжӣҙе№ҙжңҹз—ҮзҠ¶еҮәзҺ°зҡ„ж—¶еҖҷпјҢиҰҒиө°еҲ°еҢ»йҷўпјҢи®©еҢ»з”ҹи§ЈеҶі 11пјҡ01 В еҳ»е“Ҳпјҡ30+д»ҘеҗҺжҲ‘жӣҙеҠ иҮӘз”ұ 12пјҡ44 В дёүејҹпјҡжҜҸдёӘе№ҙзәӘйғҪжңүиҮӘе·ұзҡ„и§үеҜҹе’Ңз»ҸйӘҢ 15пјҡ02 В йқҷе§җпјҡ40+еҘіжҖ§еңЁз”ҹжҙ»ж–№ејҸдёҠиҰҒжҸҗж—©и°ғж•ҙпјҢеә”еҜ№еҚіе°ҶеҲ°жқҘзҡ„жӣҙе№ҙжңҹ 16пјҡ26 В йўңжҖЎ/йўңжӮҰпјҡеҰҲеҰҲзҡ„е°ұеҢ»дҪ“йӘҢеҫҲе·®пјҢеҺ»еҢ»йҷўеҰӮеҗҢеҸӮеҠ жңүеҘ–з«һзҢң 17пјҡ27 В вҖңеҸ«дёҚйҶ’вҖқзҡ„дәІжңӢеҘҪеҸӢпјҡдёәд»Җд№ҲеҰҲеҰҲд»¬дёҚзӣёдҝЎжҲ‘д»¬пјҹ 19пјҡ18 В йқҷе§җпјҡжӣҙе№ҙжңҹи®©жҲ‘еғҸзј–еү§дёҖж ·пјҢејҖе§ӢдёҚж–ӯжҖқиҖғжҲ‘зҡ„дәәз”ҹ 21пјҡ03 В йқ’жҳҘжңҹдёҺжӣҙе№ҙжңҹпјҢиў«иҚ·е°”и’ҷжҺ§еҲ¶зҡ„еҘіжҖ§ 23пјҡ00 дҪ еҸҜд»ҘжҒЁиҝҷдёӘдё–з•ҢдҪҶиҰҒзҲұиҮӘе·ұпјҒ 24пјҡ22 В йқҷе§җпјҡдәәиҰҒж•ўдәҺеҜ№иҮӘе·ұд»ҳеҮәеҫҲеӨҡзҡ„дёңиҘҝеҸ«еҒң 25пјҡ01 В йўңжҖЎ/йўңжӮҰпјҡзӨҫдјҡи§„и®ӯдёҺжҲ‘зҡ„еҘҮжҖқеҰҷжғі 25пјҡ41 В еҳ»е“ҲпјҡжҲ‘еңЁзүӣзІӘдёҠзқЎдәҶдёҖдёӢеҚҲ 29пјҡ40 В дёүејҹпјҡе№ҙиҪ»зҡ„ж„ҹеҸ—жҲ‘дҪ“йӘҢиҝҮдәҶпјҢжҲ‘дёҚеҶҚзқҖжҖҘ 30пјҡ30 В еҘіз”ҹзҡ„жҜҸдёҖеІҒйғҪиҰҒи®ӨиҜҶиҮӘе·ұпјҢжҺҘеҸ—иҮӘе·ұпјҢзҲұиҮӘе·ұ 34пјҡ05 В еҘідәәеҲ°дәҶжӣҙе№ҙжңҹд№ӢеҗҺдҪ зҡ„иә«дҪ“жүҚз¬¬дёҖж¬ЎеұһдәҺиҮӘе·ұ 36пјҡ56 В иҝҷдёӘж—¶д»Јзҡ„еҘіжҖ§дјҡжңүжӣҙеҲӣж–°зҡ„жҙ»жі• 38пјҡ31 жҲ‘жҠҠиҮӘе·ұж”ҫеңЁдәәз”ҹзҡ„з¬¬дёҖдҪҚ 40пјҡ37 В еҘідәәдёҚйңҖиҰҒеҜ№д»»дҪ•дәәж„ҹеҲ°жҠұжӯү 41пјҡ25 Endingпјҡж— и®әдәәз”ҹзҡ„е“ӘдёӘйҳ¶ж®өпјҢжҲ‘д»¬йғҪиҰҒзҲұиҮӘе·ұ гҖҗиҠұзө®еҪ©иӣӢгҖ‘ дёүејҹпјҲи„ұеҸЈз§Җжј”е‘ҳпјҢзј–еү§пјү еҳ»е“ҲпјҲи„ұеҸЈз§Җжј”е‘ҳпјү жӯҘжғҠдә‘пјҲи„ұеҸЈз§Җжј”е‘ҳпјү йўңжҖЎ/йўңжӮҰпјҲи„ұеҸЈз§Җжј”е‘ҳпјү еңҶж»Ўз»“жқҹ! гҖҗеҳүе®ҫеҲҶдә«д№ҰзұҚгҖ‘ гҖҠй—ӯз»Ҹи®°гҖӢ гҖҗж—ҘгҖ‘дјҠи—ӨжҜ”еҗ•зҫҺ гҖҠдёҚжҳҜдҪ пјҢжҳҜдҪ зҡ„иҚ·е°”и’ҷгҖӢгҖҗиӢұгҖ‘еҰ®еҹәВ·еЁҒе»үе§Ҷж–Ҝ гҖҠAll FoursеӣӣиӮўзқҖең°гҖӢгҖҗзҫҺгҖ‘зұіе…°иҫҫВ·иЈҳдёҪ гҖҗжң¬жңҹйҹід№җгҖ‘ гҖҠI Am WomanгҖӢCynthia Nixon гҖҠ20 30 40гҖӢеј иүҫеҳүгҖҒеҲҳиӢҘиӢұгҖҒжқҺеҝғжҙҒ гҖҗе…ідәҺеҗ¬еҸӢзҫӨгҖ‘ еӨ§е®¶еҸҜд»ҘйҖҡиҝҮжү«з Ғе’Ңжҗңзҙўni_jingbuxialai ж·»еҠ е°ҸеҠ©зҗҶйӮҖиҜ·еӨ§е®¶иҝӣзҫӨгҖӮ еңЁзҫӨйҮҢеӨ§е®¶еҸҜд»Ҙпјҡ 1.й’ҲеҜ№еҸ‘еёғеҪ“жңҹеҶ…е®№иҜқйўҳиҝӣиЎҢж·ұеәҰи®Ёи®ә 2.еҸӮдёҺиҠӮзӣ®иҜқйўҳе’Ңдә’еҠЁй—®йўҳзҡ„еҫҒйӣҶ 3.еҲҶдә«е…ідәҺиҠӮзӣ®еҶ…е®№е’ҢеҪўејҸзҡ„е»әи®® 4.е®ҡжңҹеҸ‘ж”ҫзҫӨзҰҸеҲ© еҗ¬еҸӢзҫӨејҖж”ҫе…ҘзҫӨж—¶й—ҙдёәжҜҸе‘ЁдёҖпјҢе‘Ёдә”зҡ„10:30пҪһ12:30пјҢж„ҹи°ўеӨ§е®¶еҜ№жҲ‘д»¬зҡ„ж”ҜжҢҒгҖӮ жң¬жңҹиҠӮзӣ®жҳҜдёүеҘҪе§җеҰ№ж’ӯе®ўдјҒеҲ’гҖҢеҘіеӯҗз¬‘жңүеҠӣйҮҸгҖҚзі»еҲ—иҠӮзӣ®д№ӢдёҖпјҢж¬ўиҝҺж”¶еҗ¬жң¬ж¬ЎдјҒеҲ’зҡ„е…¶д»–иҠӮзӣ®пјҢд»Ҙй”ӢеҲ©еҸҲиҪ»зӣҲзҡ„е§ҝжҖҒпјҢз•…иҒҠеҘіжҖ§ж—Ҙеёёз»ҸйӘҢйҮҢзҡ„зғҰжҒјдёҺеӣ°жғ‘пјҢи®©еҘ№зҡ„з¬‘еЈ°еҠӣйҮҸж— йҷҗе»¶з»ӯгҖӮ title: [з•ӘеӨ–-зӢӮе–ңж’ӯе®ўиҠӮ]еҜ№иҜқиҗҪж—Ҙд№ӢеҗҺ-зҙ дәәеҒҡж’ӯе®ўеҲ°еә•жҖҺд№ҲдёӘдәӢе„ҝ
summary: иҝҷжңҹйҹійў‘еҪ•еҲ¶дәҺ2025.5.10пјҢзӢӮе–ңж’ӯе®ўиҠӮеҜ№и°ҲзҺ°еңәпјҢйқһеёёејҖеҝғиғҪеҸӮеҠ ж’ӯе®ўдё»ж’ӯе’Ңеҗ¬еҸӢзҡ„зәҝдёӢеӨ§еӣўе»әпјҢж„ҹи°ўдё»еҠһж–№е…ійӣ…иҚ»иҖҒеёҲзҡ„йӮҖиҜ·пјҢи®©жҲ‘д»¬и§ҒеҲ°дәҶеҫҲеӨҡе–ңж¬ўзҡ„дё»ж’ӯгҖӮ пјҲе–ңж¬ўеҗ¬ж•…дәӢе’ҢжЎҲд»¶зҡ„жңӢеҸӢеҸҜд»Ҙз•ҘиҝҮиҝҷжңҹе“ҲпјҒпјү иҝҷеңәжҳҜз”ұгҖҠиҗҪж—Ҙд№ӢеҗҺгҖӢзҡ„дё»ж’ӯж №еҸ·д№ҷгҖҒйҮ‘йҫҷйұјпјҢд»ҘеҸҠжҲ‘д»¬пјҲе°ҸзҫҠе’ҢйҮ‘йұјпјүи®Ёи®әеҶіе®ҡзҡ„дё»йўҳпјҡгҖҠзҙ дәәеҒҡж’ӯе®ўеҲ°еә•иЎҢдёҚиЎҢпјҹзҺ°еңәз»ҷеӨ§е®¶иЎЁжј”жү“йҖҖе Ӯйј“пјҒгҖӢ жҲ‘д»¬д»ҺеҒҡж’ӯе®ўзҡ„иө·еӣ ејҖе§ӢиҒҠпјҢиҜҙеҲ°иҝҮзЁӢдёӯзҡ„еҝ«д№җе’Ңж— еҘҲгҖҒз—ӣиӢҰпјҢеҲҶдә«дәҶжҲ‘д»¬зҡ„з»ҸйӘҢе’ҢзңӢжі•пјҢеёҢжңӣеҜ№жғіиҰҒејҖе§ӢеҒҡж’ӯе®ўзҡ„жңӢеҸӢиғҪжңүдёҖзӮ№зӮ№её®еҠ©гҖӮ еӣӣдҪҚжҳҜд»Җд№ҲеҘ‘жңәејҖе§ӢеҒҡж’ӯе®ўзҡ„пјҹ дёӯй—ҙжңүд»Җд№Ҳи®°еҝҶж·ұеҲ»зҡ„еҘҪиҜ„е’ҢжҒ¶иҜ„пјҹ е“Әдәӣж—¶еҲ»жғіж”ҫејғпјҹеҒҡж’ӯе®ўеҜ№иҮӘе·ұжңүе“ӘдәӣеҪұе“Қпјҹ еҒҡж’ӯе®ўеҲ°еә•жҢЈй’ұеҗ—пјҹе…·дҪ“жҖҺд№Ҳз®—зҡ„пјҹ еӨ§зәІеҶҷеӨҡе°‘пјҹиө„ж–ҷд»Һе“ӘжқҘпјҹеҪ•еҲ¶зҡ„ж—¶еҖҷжңҖжҖ•д»Җд№Ҳпјҹ вҖңи®Іж•…дәӢвҖқзҡ„иғҪеҠӣжҳҜйҖҡз”Ёиҙ§еёҒпјҒ вҖңеҒҡж’ӯе®ўжІЎй—Ёж§ӣпјҹвҖқвҖ”вҖ”еҜ№е•ҠпјҒжІЎй—Ёж§ӣжҳҜеҘҪдәӢдёҖжЎ©пјҒ еҗҺеҚҠеңәиҒҠеҲ°дәҶжҲ‘д»¬еӣӣдёӘзҲұиҜҙвҖңз®—дәҶвҖқзҡ„дәәпјҢйғҪдјҡеңЁд»Җд№Ҳж—¶еҖҷжү“йҖҖе Ӯйј“пјҢжҲ‘д»¬еҺҹжң¬д»ҘдёәеҜ№дәҺиҝҷдёӘиҜқйўҳпјҢжҲ‘д»¬дјҡеҠқеӨ§е®¶еҲ«еҒҡпјҢдҪҶжҳҜиҒҠеӨ©еҜ№и°Ҳзҡ„йӯ…еҠӣе°ұеңЁдәҺжӯӨпјҢжҜҸдёӘдәәйғҪеңЁе®һж—¶зҡ„жҖқиҖғе’ҢеҸ‘еҮәи§ӮзӮ№пјҢжңҖз»ҲжҲ‘д»¬еұ…з„¶иҫҫжҲҗе…ұиҜҶпјҢзңҹзҡ„еҫҲеёҢжңӣиғҪжңүжӣҙеӨҡзҡ„жңӢеҸӢеҸҜд»ҘйҖҡиҝҮж’ӯе®ўиҝҷдёӘжё йҒ“жқҘи®°еҪ•иҮӘе·ұзҡ„жғіжі•е’ҢеЈ°йҹігҖӮ жңҖеҗҺиҰҒиҜҙжҳҺпјҢжңҹй—ҙжҲ‘д»¬иҜ»зҡ„жҳҜзҺ°еңәи§Ӯдј—зҡ„е°ҸзәёжқЎжҸҗй—®пјҢжҲ‘д»¬жқҘеҒҡи§Јзӯ”пјҢеҰӮжһңжӮЁж”¶еҗ¬зҡ„иҝҮзЁӢдёӯжҒ°еҘҪд№ҹжңүеҗҢж ·зҡ„й—®йўҳеҸҜд»ҘйЎәзқҖеҗ¬пјҢеҰӮжһңдёҚж„ҹе…ҙи¶ЈеҸҜд»Ҙеҝ«иҝӣе“ҰгҖӮд№ҹжңүзҺ°еңәи§Ӯдј—зҡ„еҸ‘иЁҖеЈ°йҹіпјҢжҖ•еӨ§е®¶дјҡи§үеҫ—зӘҒз„¶еӨҡдәҶеҲ«дәәжңүзӮ№ж··д№ұжҸҗеүҚиҜҙжҳҺдёҖдёӢ~ жҲ‘д»¬еҫҲејҖеҝғиғҪжңүиҝҷж ·еҒҡжҙ»дәәзҡ„жңәдјҡпјҢеёҢжңӣеӨ§е®¶д№ҹиғҪе–ңж¬ўгҖӮеҪ“з„¶еҰӮжһңеӨ§е®¶жңүд»»дҪ•й—®йўҳпјҢж¬ўиҝҺеңЁиҜ„и®әеҢәе’ҢжҲ‘д»¬дәӨжөҒпјҒ еҶҚж¬Ўж„ҹи°ўзӢӮе–ңж’ӯе®ўиҠӮпјҒеҒҡж’ӯе®ўзңҹжҳҜеӨӘеҘҪе•ҰпјҒ BGMпјҡ Brandenburg Concerto No.3 In G BWV 10483. Allegro - Hedwig Bilgram,Karl Richter,MГјnchener Bach-Orchester

title: Are Mast Cells Driving Autism? with Dr. Pejman Katiraei  (Ep 145)
podcast_name: Bendy Bodies with Dr. Linda Bluestein
summary: What if the diagnosis was just the beginningвҖ”and the root cause was hiding in plain sight? In this riveting episode, Dr. Linda Bluestein welcomes pediatrician and integrative medicine expert Dr. Pejman Katiraei, whose work with children struggling with autism, mold exposure, and mast cell activation reveals a shocking truth: many kids arenвҖҷt just neurodivergentвҖ”they have neuroinflammation. Together, they peel back the layers on whatвҖҷs really drivingВ autistic behaviors, why some children canвҖҷt tolerate food, noise, or even hugsвҖ”and how mast cells and histamine might be behind it all. Dr. KatiraeiВ sharesВ remarkable stories of transformation, explains why traditional testing often fails, and reveals a controversial treatment thatвҖҷs changing lives. If your child is sensitive, reactive, or struggling to connectвҖ”and youвҖҷve been told itвҖҷs just "behavioral"вҖ”this conversation might rewrite what you thought was possible. Takeaways:    One child's refusal to eat broccoli held more answers than anyone expected.    Could colic be the first warning sign of central inflammation?    He couldnвҖҷt feel loveвҖ”until one unlikely medication changed everything.    A house tested cleanвҖҰ but the child kept getting worse. Why?    Some diagnoses miss whatвҖҷs burning beneath the surface. title: In the Netherlands: a day in a GP practice (Part 1)
podcast_name: Primary Focus with Dr. Tara Kiran
summary: How is it that 99% of people in the Netherlands have access to primary care? How can Dutch doctors look after large numbers of patients while still providing primary care? Those are some of the questions Dr. Kiran is set on answering when she visits the Netherlands to learn more about their primary care system. In the first of this four part series, Dr. Kiran brings you with her as she visits a GP practice in Nijmegen. She shadows Dr. Tim Olde Hartmen and Dr. Suzanne Ligthart to understand a typical work day. She meets key members of their team and unpacks how they all work together. In Part Two, Dr. Kiran introduces you to what she called вҖңthe secret sauceвҖқ of the Dutch primary care system. Stay tuned! See a gallery of Dr. KiranвҖҷs photos from her trip to the Netherlands. Read Dr. KiranвҖҷs 4-part series on the Netherlands published in the Medical Post and the insights she shared with Andre Picard at the Globe and Mail. Listen to Dr. Kiran speaking about her trip on CBCвҖҷs White Coat, Black Art Read an article about TaraвҖҷs host, Dr. Tim Olde Hartman: A Giant Standing on the Shoulders of Giants Subscribe to our Substack newsletter to get bonus content. Take the OurCare national survey to share your experiences with primary care. Do you have an idea for an episode? Email primary.focus@unityhealth.toВ  Dr. Tara Kiran is a family physician and researcher who is passionate about building a stronger, more equitable primary care system in Canada. She practices at St. MichaelвҖҷs Hospital, Unity Health Toronto where she is also a scientist at MAP Centre for Urban Health Solutions. In 2022, Dr. Kiran launched OurCare, a national initiative to engage the public in shaping the future of primary care in Canada. You can learn more about Dr. KiranвҖҷs research here: https://maphealth.ca/kiran/В  Primary Focus is supported by a grant from the St. MichaelвҖҷs Foundation. Dr. Tara Kiran is supported as the Fidani Chair in Improvement and Innovation at the University of Toronto and a Scientist in the Departments of Family and Community Medicine at St. MichaelвҖҷs Hospital and the University of Toronto.   This is a public episode. If you would like to discuss this with other subscribers or get access to bonus episodes, visit primaryfocus.substack.com

title: зҺ°еңәпјҒзҺ°еңәпјҒ27 | еӣҪе®үи¶ізҗғе°Ҹе°Ҷд№Ӣжӯ»пјҡеҢ—жјӮжө·ж·Җе®¶еәӯдёҺз ҙзўҺзҡ„и¶…зә§зҗғе‘ҳжўҰ
summary: дё»ж’ӯ | еҗҙдёҪзҺ®пјҢгҖҠдёүиҒ”з”ҹжҙ»е‘ЁеҲҠгҖӢдё»д»»и®°иҖ…еҳүе®ҫ | зҺӢжҖЎз„¶пјҢгҖҠдёүиҒ”з”ҹжҙ»е‘ЁеҲҠгҖӢи®°иҖ… 2025е№ҙ2жңҲ6ж—ҘпјҢеңЁиҘҝзҸӯзүҷй©¬еҫ·йҮҢдёҫеҠһзҡ„дёҖеңәжҜ”иөӣдёӯпјҢеҢ—дә¬еӣҪе®үи¶ізҗғдҝұд№җйғЁйқ’и®ӯжўҜйҳҹе°Ҹе°Ҷйғӯеҳүз’ҮеңЁй“Ізҗғж—¶йҒӯйҒҮж„ҸеӨ–йҮҚдјӨпјҢиў«иҜҠж–ӯдёәи„‘жӯ»дәЎгҖӮ йғӯеҳүз’ҮжҳҜеҢ—дә¬еӣҪе®үдҝұд№җйғЁU21жўҜйҳҹзҡ„дёӯеҗҺеҚ«пјҢжӯӨж¬ЎжҳҜдҪңдёәе…ЁиҝҗдјҡеҢ—дә¬U20д»ЈиЎЁйҳҹзҡ„дёҖе‘ҳпјҢеүҚеҫҖиҘҝзҸӯзүҷеҸӮеҠ еҶ¬и®ӯгҖӮеңЁж„ҸеӨ–еҸ‘з”ҹеҗҺпјҢйғӯеҳүз’Үзҡ„е®¶дәәйҖүжӢ©е°Ҷд»–еёҰеӣһеҢ—дә¬з»§з»ӯж•‘жІ»гҖӮйҒ—жҶҫзҡ„жҳҜпјҢеңЁ2025е№ҙ3жңҲ19ж—ҘжҷҡпјҢйғӯеҳүз’ҮеңЁеҢ—дә¬еӨ©еқӣеҢ»йҷўеӣ жҠўж•‘ж— ж•ҲеҺ»дё–гҖӮиҝҷдёҖеӨ©д№ҹжҳҜд»–18еІҒзҡ„жңҖеҗҺдёҖеӨ©гҖӮ еңЁжӮІеү§еҸ‘з”ҹеүҚпјҢйғӯеҳүз’ҮжҳҜеҗҢе№ҙйҫ„ж®өеӣҪеҶ…йқ’и®ӯзҗғе‘ҳдёӯзҡ„дҪјдҪјиҖ…пјҢжӣҫдҪңдёәе”ҜдёҖдёҖеҗҚдёӯеӣҪзҗғе‘ҳе…ҘйҖүиҝҮ2023е№ҙзҡ„жӢңд»Ғдё–з•ҢйҳҹеҗҚеҚ•гҖӮд»ҺвҖңU12вҖқжҲҗй•ҝеҲ°вҖңU21вҖқпјҢе·Із»ҸдёӨж¬Ўе…ҘйҖүеӣҪе®үйў„еӨҮйҳҹзҡ„йғӯеҳүз’Үи·қзҰ»жҲҗдёәдёҖеҗҚиҒҢдёҡзҗғе‘ҳпјҢд№ҹеҸӘжңүдёҖжӯҘд№ӢйҒҘгҖӮ иҖҢд»ҺиҒҡеұ…зқҖеӨ–жқҘеҠЎе·Ҙдәәе‘ҳзҡ„еҢ—дә¬йҮ‘е®¶жқ‘еҲ°еӣҪе®үйқ’и®ӯйҳҹпјҢйғӯеҳүз’Үзҡ„е°‘е№ҙи¶ізҗғжўҰд№ҹжҳҜдёҖдёӘеҢ—жјӮе®¶еәӯзҡ„вҖңеҢ—дә¬жўҰвҖқгҖӮеңЁиҝҷжқЎйҖҗжўҰд№Ӣи·ҜдёҠпјҢиҝҷдёӘе®¶еәӯжңүзқҖе·ЁеӨ§зҡ„д»ҳеҮәпјҢд№ҹеҜ№жңӘжқҘжңүиҝҮзҫҺеҘҪзҡ„жҶ§жҶ¬пјҢдҪҶеӣ дёәиҝҷеңәж„ҸеӨ–пјҢдёҖеҲҮжҲӣз„¶иҖҢжӯўгҖӮ title: Episode 50 - How social pressure can affect your health choices and what to do about it.
summary: One of things many of my accountability clients struggle with is the social pressure from others to step out of their health commitments.В  В This often looks like consuming food or alcohol that is not aligned with their health goals.В  В I have experienced this myself and know how tough it can be.В В  В  This podcast addresses the 'good' and 'bad' social pressures and then gives some strategies to help with sticking to your goals when others try and derail you...В  How to respond and stand up for what you believe in: Find like-minded people Explain with confidence and be assertive - no thanks and not for me Think of an excuse Suggest something else to do Avoid certain situations Stand up for others Ask questions of the other person вҖ“ deflect Be repetitive Consider the results of giving in Seek support вҖ“ therapy В  Enjoy and thank you for listening..

title: Tracer Fire; I Sorta Took That Personally Episode 159
summary: In this riveting episode of So There I Was, we sit down with John вҖңApache 2-3вҖқ Haugen, a Vietnam-era Army aviator with stories that span from the draft line in 1968 to combat flying in the Mekong Delta. John shares how he dodged a ground-pounder fate by heading to Officer Candidate School, ultimately flying slicks and accumulating over 900 hours of combat flight time. With humor, humility, and gripping detail, he recounts narrow escapes, night missions, forced landings, and his close call in a mangrove swamp that led to a surreal вҖңthird manвҖқ experience. From learning in OH-23s and flying Hueys to coordinating strikes with Cobras and Puff the Magic Dragon, JohnвҖҷs stories highlight the courage, camaraderie, and absurdities of combat aviation in Vietnam. Bonus tales include bar tabs in Manhattan, unexpected rat-kebabs, and the elusive вҖңHawk'sвҖқ little black book. This is an episode packed with history, grit, and a whole lot of heart!

title: Jim Morrison, Secret Teacher Of The Occult w/Paul Wyld
summary: Darkness Radio Presents: Jim Morrison, Secret Teacher Of The Occult w/ Musician/Poet/Author, Paul Wyld! The groundbreaking 1960s band The Doors, named for Aldous HuxleyвҖҷs The Doors of Perception, achieved incredible acclaim and influence, ultimately serving as a key group in the development of psychedelic and progressive rock. At the center of it all was complex front man Jim Morrison, who died in 1971 at only 27. Yet, as author Paul Wyld reveals, despite MorrisonвҖҷs reputation as a lewd, drunken performer, he was a full- fledged mystical, shamanic figure, a secret teacher of the occult who was not merely central to the development of rock music, but to the growth of the Western esoteric tradition as a whole. Wyld looks at the mystical works that inspired Morrison, including Kurt SeligmannвҖҷs The Mirror of Magic, Colin WilsonвҖҷs The Outsider, and the writings of Nietzsche and Jack Kerouac. Drawing on MorrisonвҖҷs lyrics and poems, his intimate writings, and the recollections of friends like photographer Paul Ferrara and Doors keyboard player Ray Manzarek, the author makes the case that Morrison was not simply a superficial dabbler in the occult but an actual secret teacher transmitting knowledge through the golden thread stretching back to Egypt and Thoth-Hermes. On Today's Darkness Radio, we talk with Paul about Morrison's journey before the Doors, what events shaped his life to make him set out on an esoteric journey.В  We discuss the parallels between the movie Lawrence of Arabia and Morrison's journey and why heВ  may have taken cues from that movie on his spiritual journey.В  We also discuss significant spiritual highpoint's of Morrison's life, his tragic end, and much more! You'll learn:  - Top signs youвҖҷre entering perimenopause (hint: itвҖҷs not just about missing periods)  - The big lifestyle shifts that move the needle for sleep, weight management, and long-term health  - How to advocate for yourself when talking to your doctor (and where to turn if youвҖҷre not getting the help you need)  - Why muscle gain, strength training, and smart nutrition are non-negotiable  - How to balance hormonal changes without losing your mindвҖ”or your marriage!В   If youвҖҷre approaching 40, deep in the menopause transition, or simply want to feel empowered (instead of blindsided), this episode will leave you laughing, learning, and ready to take action. title: The Big Changes That Happen When You Eat Healthy For Just Two Weeks | Dr. Neal Barnard
podcast_name: The Exam Room by the Physicians Committee
summary: Your body reacts to a healthy diet far faster than numbers fall on a scale.  В   Long before you begin losing weight your body begins to transform radically inside. One of the biggest benefits is dramatically reduced cellular inflammation. This subsequently lowers the risk of chronic diseases, including heart disease and type 2 diabetes.  В   Dr. Neal Barnard reviews a South African study showing how quickly the benefits of a healthy diet can take shape as he joins Chuck Carroll on The Exam Room Podcast.  В   The study examines people who are put on a predominantly whole food plant-based diet for two weeks and compares them to those eating a popular higher fat Western diet, and vice versa. The results are incredible!  В   This episode is sponsored by The Gregory J. title: ENCORE! #287: Everything YouвҖҷve Been Taught About Digital Clubbing is Wrong

summary: Every other week I'm republishing one of my most popular or impactful episodes and adding an update, new insight, or context that will help you benefit from it even more. This week I'm highlighting Episode 287, which is one of my all-time favorite episodes. And thatвҖҷs because it completely debunks some things I (and many students) are taught in nursing school. I learned a ton creating this episode, still re-listen to it on occasion, and hope you find it just as valuable, too! In nursing school, I learned that digital clubbing was a key sign of COPD and I accepted this as gospel and moved on with my life. I used this information to answer exam questions on the topic and I always included assessing for it in my care plans for patients with COPD. But, like all things in nursing, thereвҖҷs so much more to this story. In this episode youвҖҷll learn why much of what youвҖҷve been taught about digital clubbing is wrong, and whatвҖҷs actually going on (spoiler alertвҖҰwe donвҖҷt fully know!) title: Episode 466: Enid Bush
summary: In this episode of Intermittent Fasting Stories, Gin talks to Enid Bush from Manalapan, NJ. Are you ready to take your intermittent fasting lifestyle to the next level? ThereвҖҷs nothing better than community to help with that.В In the Delay, DonвҖҷt Deny community we all embrace the clean fast, and thereвҖҷs just the right support for you as you live your intermittent fasting lifestyle.В  You can connect directly with Gin in the Ask Gin group, and she will answer all of your questions personally.В If youвҖҷre new to intermittent fasting or recommitting to the IF lifestyle, join the 28-Day FAST Start group.В After your fast start, join us for support in The 1st Year group.В Need tips for long term maintenance? We have a place for that!В There are many more useful spaces beyond these, and you can interact in as many as you like.В  Visit ginstephens.com/community to join us. An annual membership costs just over a dollar a week when you do the math.В If you arenвҖҷt ready to fully commit for a year, join for a month and you can cancel at any time. If you know youвҖҷll want to stay forever, we also have a lifetime membership option available.В В  IF is free. You donвҖҷt need to join our community to fast. But if youвҖҷre looking for support from a community of like-minded IFers, we are here for you atВ ginstephens.com/community.В  Enid is a retired teacher, currently teaching theater to young children, and she also works as a health coach.В Enid shares her journey with intermittent fasting, which began when she discovered a health coach named Dr. Livingood, who introduced her to the health benefits of fasting. Inspired by his insights about autophagy, and encouraged by a nutritionist to work out on an empty stomach, Enid began fasting. However, it wasn't until she stumbled upon Gin's book, Fast Feast Repeat, that she realized she wasn't fasting clean, leading her to a deeper understanding and better implementation of the lifestyle. Enid discusses her struggles with diets in the past, detailing how she spent years trying various diets like Fit for Life, Herbalife, Weight Watchers, and different nutrition programs, only to find them restrictive and unsustainable. After beginning the clean fast on January 10, 2023, Enid noticed improvements in her well-being and recently celebrated two years in the lifestyle. Throughout the conversation, Enid emphasizes the transformation in her relationship with food. She highlights the joy of enjoying meals more thoroughly and describes how her tastes have naturally shifted towards healthier options, allowing herself to be more selective with what she eats. The episode also touches on her experiences with social expectations and perceptions about fasting, how she responds to skepticism from others, and the importance of understanding one's own body through personalized nutrition and fasting practices. Enid concludes by sharing her ultimate advice to those new to intermittent fasting: recognize the advantages of fasting beyond weight loss, particularly its healing potential. She advises newcomers to have patience, give their bodies time to adjust, focus on a clean fast, and believe in themselves. Emphasizing the freedom that comes with trusting one's own hunger signals and preferences, Enid advocates for a shift away from societal diet pressures and towards a more intuitive, satisfying eating experience. You can find Enid at embhealthcoach.com. Get GinвҖҷs books at: https://www.ginstephens.com/get-the-books.html. Good news!В The second edition of Delay, DonвҖҷt Deny is now available in ebook, paperback, hardback, and audiobook.В This is the book that youвҖҷll want to start with or share with others, as it is a simple introduction to IF.В ItвҖҷs been updated to include the clean fast, an easier to understand and more thorough description of ADF and all of your ADF options, and an all new success stories section.В When shopping, make sure to get the second edition, which has a 2024 publication date.В The audiobook for the second edition is available now!В  Join GinвҖҷs community! Go to: ginstephens.com/community Do you enjoy Intermittent Fasting Stories?В YouвҖҷll probably also like GinвҖҷs other podcast with cohost Sheri Bullock:В Fast. Feast. Repeat. Intermittent Fasting for Life.В Find it wherever you listen to podcasts.В В  Share your intermittent fasting stories with Gin: gin@intermittentfastingstories.com Visit GinвҖҷs website at: ginstephens.comВ  Check out GinвҖҷs Favorite Things at http://www.ginstephens.com/gins-favorite-things.html See Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.

title: Episode 7: One Mistake Is All It Takes: Real Stories from NZ's Deadliest Waters
summary: Send us a text The West Coast of New Zealand offers world-class fishing but demands extreme respect, with veterans sharing hard-earned wisdom on staying safe in these challenging waters. Five local legends reveal how they've survived decades fishing this untamed coastline while witnessing the sometimes fatal consequences of poor preparation and decision-making. вҖў West Coast fishing described as "second to none" but protected by dangerous conditions вҖў Fishery relatively untapped compared to East Coast due to challenging weather and access вҖў No islands or sheltered harbours to provide safety when conditions deteriorateВ  вҖў Drowning statistics reveal 192 recreational fisher deaths since 1980, with 66% occurring on rocks вҖў Only 26% of rock fishers wear life jackets, while 36% can't swim more than 50 meters вҖў Proper safety gear includes fitted life jackets, appropriate footwear (not gumboots), PLBs вҖў Fishing with mates creates crucial safety network for emergencies вҖў Local fishing clubs provide vital knowledge, assistance and community safety systems вҖў Bar crossings require careful planning around tides, swells and wind conditions вҖў Recovery efforts for drowning victims create lasting trauma for rescuers and families If you're heading out fishing, check weather forecasts, wear appropriate safety gear, tell someone your plans, and remember - no fish is worth your life. title: Rock Bottom to Redemption with Niall Harbison
summary: Send us a text "What if your darkest moment became the catalyst for your greatest transformation?" This question lies at the heart of my profound conversation with Niall Harbison, whose remarkable journey from corporate success to rock-bottom alcoholism to purposeful revival might just change your perspective on what truly matters in life. Niall's story begins as a private chef for billionaires aboard luxury yachts, where million-dollar fuel bills and private jets delivering Italian cheese were commonplace. He witnessed firsthand the hollow nature of extreme wealth before returning to Dublin to build his own successful businesses. Yet beneath this veneer of achievement lurked depression, anxiety, and a growing dependence on alcohol. When COVID struck, Niall's life spiraled into a six-week bender in Thailand that nearly killed him. Lying in a hospital bed, certain he was dying, he experienced a revelation that would transform everything: the corporate success, social media followers, and material possessions he'd chased meant absolutely nothing. What flashed through his mind instead were simple moments of childhood joy and connection. That rock-bottom moment became what Niall now calls "the best thing in my life." Upon recovery, he discovered purpose in rescuing Thailand's street dogs. What began as feeding a few hungry canines has expanded into a charity that feeds 1,200 dogs daily, performs thousands of sterilizations monthly, and has built a hospital named after Tina, a special rescue who lived just six months in freedom after a lifetime of abuse. Niall's transformation at age 41 stands as powerful testament that it's never too late to reinvent yourself. His advice resonates with brutal honesty: "Follow your own path. Take risks. You don't have to do the life that's mapped out for you." Whether you're questioning your purpose or just need a reminder of what truly matters, this conversation might be exactly what you need to hear. Subscribe now and join the thousands finding inspiration in these authentic human stories of transformation. Thanks for listening! You can watch the full episode on YouTube here. DonвҖҷt forget to follow The Laura Dowling Experience podcast on Instagram @lauradowlingexperience for updates and more information. You can also follow our host, Laura Dowling, @fabulouspharmacist for more insights and tips. If you enjoyed this episode, please subscribe and leave a reviewвҖ”it really helps us out! Stay tuned for more great conversations.

From viral videos on dismantling patriarchal parenting to surviving chaotic family traditions, Payal shares what itвҖҷs like to raise sons in a world obsessed with вҖңmamaвҖҷs boys.вҖқ She even dishes on the unexpected wedding sabotage she experienced firsthand. This episode dives deep into family roles, wedding faux pas, and why boundaries are the real bridal registry essential. Get ready to rethink whatвҖҷs вҖңnormalвҖқ at the altar and beyond. Join me on Patreon and get bonus content every month!В  Must-Hear Insights and Key Moments Dusty Son Series Origins вҖ“ Payal unpacks the viral moment that sparked her mission to dismantle toxic gender expectations.The Color Clash вҖ“ Someone close to Payal showed up to her wedding wearing her exact dress colorвҖ”after being told not to.Wedding Traditions That Go Off the Rails вҖ“ Learn how fun customs can turn violent when underlying family tensions boil over.Cake Smash or Red Flag? вҖ“ Why some вҖңplayfulвҖқ wedding moments are actually warning signs.Mother of the Groom Drama вҖ“ A deep dive into the overbearing вҖңboy momвҖқ trope and why it needs to stop.Navigating Internalized Misogyny вҖ“ Payal shares how her early marriage exposed unexpected insecuritiesвҖ”and how she overcame them.About Payal Payal Desai, known online as @Payalforstyle on Instagram and TikTok, is the creator of the viral вҖңDusty SonвҖқ seriesвҖ”a hilarious and honest look at breaking down traditional gender roles, starting at home with her own sons. Her content has racked up millions of likes and caught the attention of major media outlets and talk shows for its bold take on everyday dynamics within families. A sharp-witted cultural commentator, Payal uses humor to spotlight the invisible labor women carry and to challenge outdated expectations with unfiltered honesty. ThereвҖҷs no dramatic backstoryвҖ”just a mom calling it like she sees it and making a lot of people laugh (and think) along the way. title: The Marathon Mindset вҖ“ Raising Children with T1D for the Long Run
summary: In this motivating and down-to-earth episode, Catherine explores what it means to parent a child with type 1 diabetes (T1D) with a marathon mindset rather than a sprint mentality. She challenges the pressures of achieving perfect blood sugar numbers and warns against the unsustainable, all-or-nothing approaches often fuelled by social media and fear. Instead, she offers a practical, compassionate framework using SMART goalsвҖ”Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Realistic, and TimelyвҖ”to encourage gradual, joyful, and sustainable diabetes management. Listeners are encouraged to shift the focus from perfection to progress, helping children learn patience, resilience, and flexibility. Key messages include: Diabetes is a lifelong journey, not a race to perfect numbers.All-or-nothing thinking can lead to guilt and burnout.SMART goals can support steady improvement without overwhelming children.Joy and balance matter вҖ” food and life should be celebrated, not restricted.Self-compassion and role-modelling are essential to raising empowered, healthy kids with T1D.CatherineвҖҷs closing message reminds parents that imperfect days are normalвҖ”and that raising a child with diabetes is not about getting everything right, but about staying in it for the long run with love, grace, and determination. The Diabetic Mammy is the heartfelt, no-nonsense podcast where type 1 diabetes meets motherhood, community, and a whole lot of real talk. Hosted by Catherine, a mammy navigating the highs and lows of type 1 diabetes, each episode blends personal insight with practical wisdom, offering support for parents, carers, and anyone living with or loving someone with diabetes. From tech transitions and hospital diagnoses to school lunches and stolen sleep, this podcast embraces the messy, meaningful moments that make up life with chronic illness. Whether youвҖҷre newly diagnosed or a seasoned warrior, The Diabetic Mammy is your space to feel seen, supported, and never alone. Connect with Catherine by email on catherine@abundantlydiabetes.com or reach her on Instagram: abundantlydiabetes  Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: GCS; Roadside to Resus
summary: Welcome back! In this episode, weвҖҷre diving deep into something we all think we know, the Glasgow Coma Scale.  The GCS has been a fundamental part of assessing patients with altered consciousness for over 50 years. YouвҖҷll find it in trauma scores, neurology exams and practically every prehospital and ED handover. But here's the thing, is it as reliable and useful as we think?  In this episode, weвҖҷll explore the origins of the scale, what it was designed for and how itвҖҷs been used (and maybe misused...) since. We take a look at how reproducible it really is, particularly when different clinicians score the same patient. Spoiler alert: itвҖҷs not always as consistent as you might hope!  WeвҖҷll also unpack the individual components; eyes, voice, motor and ask if they all carry equal weight, or are some more prognostically useful than others? Because a GCS of 4 isnвҖҷt always the same GCS of 4, depending on how you get thereвҖҰ  WeвҖҷll be looking at real-world implications, how we make decisions around airway management, imaging, and referral, all based on that one number.  So whether youвҖҷre in prehospital care, the ED, or intensive care - stick with us as we try to answer the question: is the GCS still doing what we need it to, or is it time to move on?  Once again weвҖҷd love to hear any thoughts or feedback either on the website or via X @TheResusRoom!  Simon, Rob & James

title: The Wild World of Backyard Ultras with Phil Gore
summary: In this episode of The Life Outside Podcast, Cam chats with Phil Gore. Phil is an accomplished ultramarathon runner, specialising in Backyard Ultras, a race style where runners complete 6.7km loops on the hour, every hour, until one person remains. title: The Whole Truth About Supplements: Why Wholefoods Work When Synthetics DonвҖҷt вҖ“ with Sheridan Joy Austin
podcast_name: The Wellness Collective with Nat Kringoudis
summary: LetвҖҷs talk about the supplement industryвҖ”and why so much of it is failing women. In this raw, uplifting and incredibly educational conversation, IвҖҷm joined by Sheridan Joy Austin, the beautiful mind and founder behind Foraged For You, to dive into what it really takes to nourish our bodiesвҖ”especially in pregnancy, postpartum, and perimenopause. Sheridan opens up about how her own unexpected pregnancy revealed the gaps in prenatal nutrition and how that led to the creation of her now cult-favourite wholefood blend, The MotherвҖҷs Blend. We talk liver, folate, iron, vitamin A, and why isolating nutrients in synthetic supplements often creates more harm than good. Sheridan shares her journey of bringing a product to life with true integrity, the resistance sheвҖҷs faced, and why she refuses to cut cornersвҖ”even when the wellness industry tells her to. This episode is for any woman whoвҖҷs ever questioned her supplements, felt overwhelmed by mixed health advice, or just wants to understand how to truly fuel her body with the wisdom of nature. Key Takeaways Why real food always winsвҖ”how nutrient-dense ingredients like liver and kelp naturally support pregnancy, postpartum, and hormone recovery better than synthetics. What your supplements arenвҖҷt telling youвҖ”why isolating nutrients like folic acid or iron can backfire without their critical co-factors like vitamin A, copper, and magnesium. How to read the fine printвҖ”what hidden fillers like maltodextrin and silicon dioxide are doing to your gut, and how to choose products with real integrity. Why iron deficiency might not be about ironвҖ”how symptoms like fatigue and poor immunity may be rooted in deeper imbalances most blood tests donвҖҷt show. The truth about vitamin A in pregnancyвҖ”why liver-based vitamin A isnвҖҷt the enemy, and what the research really says about birth defects and deficiency risks. title: Ep: 419 | How Suresh Dulal Became NepalвҖҷs Ironman: From Mountain Biking to Triathlons | Sushant Pradhan Podcast
summary: In this inspiring episode, we sit down with Suresh Dulal, one of NepalвҖҷs Ironman athletes, to explore his incredible journey from the rugged mountain biking trails of Nepal to completing multiple Ironman triathlons. Suresh shares how he got started in cycling in Nepal, his transition into cross country mountain biking, and how his passion evolved into a professional pursuit in the world of endurance sports. We dive deep into the world of Ironman Nepal, discussing his intense Ironman training plan, the importance of triathlon nutrition, and how high altitude training in Nepal gives Nepali athletes a natural competitive edge. Suresh opens up about his personal experiences, his dream of competing in Ironman Hawai, and how the support of family and friends fueled his success. For aspiring Nepali triathletes, Suresh provides valuable insights on choosing the right bike on a budget, the role of diet and recovery, and how to stay mentally strong during long-distance events. We also explore the growing triathlon community in Nepal, the importance of well-facilitated gyms, and his legendary 180 km ride with four-time Ironman World Champion Chrissie Wellington. GET CONNECTED WITH Suresh Dulal: Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/share/16VDeM6j9L/

title: Aviation Maintenance Training
summary: Aviation maintenance training with the Chennault Aviation Maintenance Academy Director of Education, and with the Director of Maintenance for Chennault Aviation Services. In the news, more Newark Airport air traffic control problems, a large IAG order for Boeing and Airbus jets, calls to abolish airport screening, and QatarвҖҷs B747-8 gift. Guests Michael Riccardelli, Director of Education, Chennault Aviation Maintenance Academy (CAMA), andВ  Joshua Borel, Director of Maintenance, Chennault Aviation Services. CAMA offers hands-on aviation maintenance training that combines technical excellence with real-world experience and is rooted in the legacy of General Claire Lee Chennault and the Flying Tigers. The Aviation Maintenance Technician (AMT) program is designed both for people starting a career, and for those looking to take a new direction. It provides the skills, knowledge, and certifications for success in aviation maintenance. Mike and Josh tell us about the non-profit Aerospace Maintenance Council and the Aerospace Maintenance Council Competition, which recognizes and celebrates the aviation maintenance technician. The competition is held annually in conjunction with Aviation Week NetworkвҖҷs MRO Americas and, for the first time in 2025, in conjunction with Vertical Aviation International's VERTICON. Chennault Aviation was a sponsor of both events. CAMA offers three programs: the traditional 18-month classroom program, self-paced A&P online training, and Choose Aerospace, which is offered through the Aviation Technician Education Council (ATEC) for juniors and seniors in high schools. Joshua Borel, Michael Riccardelli, and Casey Salzar. Mike and Josh explain the worldwide shortage of aviation maintenance technicians and how they are taking action to help get younger people interested in aviation. They also describe the synergy provided when one location encompasses maintenance training, flight training, and an operating airport. Michael Riccardelli was a CH-46 Sea Knight Helicopter technician in the Marine Corps and received his A&P in 1991. He assisted NASA in the Columbia recovery operations and maintained helicopters in the oil and gas industry, as well as EMS helicopters for Air Evac Lifeteam. Mike started aviation maintenance instructing in 2011. He coached student aviation maintenance skills teams for 12 years, with multiple wins in various competitions, including the Aerospace Maintenance Competition. He fielded the first all-female student aviation maintenance skills team, which took first place. Joshua Borel flew model airplanes from the age of 8. He joined the Air Force in 2011 as a C-5 Galaxy ISO (heavy inspection) Crew Chief, winning two Knuckle Buster awards. In 2016, Josh enrolled in the Aviation Institute of Maintenance, winning 1st in the 2017 PAMA Olympics, 2nd in the 2017 AMC School Category, and 1st in the 2018 AMC School Category. He graduated in February 2018 with A&P as class Valedictorian. Joshua joined Chennault Aviation while it was just a Part 61 flight school. He received his PPL in 2021 and became Director of Maintenance in 2022. He helped grow the company into not only a flight school, but a Part 135 Charter, a Part 147 A&P School, and a public airport. Aviation News Newark Liberty Airport suffers third system outage in less than 2 weeks, impacting hundreds of flights Another week of delays and cancellations caused by a system outage. This time, a 45-minute ground stop was reportedly the result of a momentary failure of a backup system. Controller staffing remains a problem, and U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy says the number of flights will be cut. IAG orders 71 long-haul jets split between Airbus and Boeing IAG announced orders for 33 aircraft from Airbus and 38 from Boeing: Thirty-two 787-10 aircraft for British Airways, twenty-one A330-900neo aircraft, six Airbus A350-900s, six A350-1000s,В and six 777-9s.

title: Episode 716: More than a side hustle, flower farming and floral design with Marly Surena-Llorens of Fenimore & Rutland
summary: https://youtu.be/uGRgC8rjB_U?si=ucehNezGBXce9kuX Marly Surena-Llorens was born and raised in the U.S., but her Haitian motherвҖҷs stories of tropical gardens filled with palms, crotons, and bougainvillea plants inspired her lifelong love of flowers. Yet, Marly says some of her most vivid floral impressions came from the BBC programs of her youth, when she watched period TV dramas in her familyвҖҷs Brooklyn apartment. Today she gardens and grows cut flowers on two residential lots in Allentown, Pennsylvania, a college town located about one hour north of Philadelphia. Marly describes herself as a вҖңfarmer who designs,вҖқ and with just a quarter of an acre, her land produces enough flowers for Fenimore & RutlandвҖҷs seasonal bouquet subscriptions, wedding florals, and design workshops. IвҖҷm so thrilled that Robin Avni and I included MarlyвҖҷs story in our new book, The Flower Farmers, and today youвҖҷll meet her for our extended interview.  Marly Surena-Llorens of Fenimore & Rutland (c) Petra Somers WeвҖҷve had a great kickoff week for the The Flower Farmers launch and the fun continues today with Marly Surena-Llorens of Fenimore & Rutland, an Allentown, Pennsylvania-based farmer-florist, our special guest. The English cottage garden style of Marly Surena-Llorens of Fenimore & Rutland (c) Petra Somers Marly and I met at an ASCFG conference several years back and I knew I wanted to include her floral journey in a chapter of The Flower Farmers. WeвҖҷre thrilled with the gorgeous photography she shared for the pages, and weвҖҷre so happy that her story is getting out into the world. Marly Surena-Llorens at home and in her cutting garden (c) Petra Somers Today, join me in a lovely and inspiring conversation with Marly as we discuss her farmer-florist business, Fenimore & Rutland, its origins and inspiration, and the way she has turned a lifelong interest in English gardens into a micro farm serving weddings, events, and everyday customers in her area. Marly Surena-Llorens of Fenimore and Rutland in her studio (c) Petra Somers Find and follow Fenimore & Rutland on Instagram and Facebook.  Meet Marly, Debra & Robin on Tuesday, June 10th at Longwood Gardens! Tues., June 10th (6-7:30 pm), KENNETT SQUARE, Pennsylvania. Longwood GardensВ hosts Robin Avni and Debra Prinzing for a lecture and book-signing about The Flower Farmers. They'll be joined by Marly Surena-Llorens of Fenimore & Rutland (Allentown, Pennsylvania) will join as their special farmer guest.В Tickets: $26-$29;В Event Details Here. Check out the full event calendar for The Flower Farmers book launch (May & June) Thank you to our Sponsors! This show is brought to you by slowflowers.com, the free, online directory to more than 700 florists, shops, and studios who design with local, seasonal and sustainable flowersВ and to the farms that grow those blooms. ItвҖҷs the conscious choice for buying and sending flowers. Thank you to our lead sponsor, Flowerbulb.eu and their U.S. lily bulb vendors. One of the most recognizable flowers in the world, the lily is a top-selling cut flower, offering long-lasting blooms, year-round availability, and a dazzling petal palette. Flowerbulb.eu has partnered with Slow Flowers to provide beautiful lily inspiration and farming resources to help growers and florists connect their customers with more lilies. Learn more at Flowerbulb.eu. Thank you to the Association of Specialty Cut Flowers. The ASCFG is a gathering place for specialty cut flower growers of all levels of experience. It is a hub of knowledge, where seasoned experts and budding enthusiasts come together to learn, share, and support one another. The ASCFG is dedicated to empowering its members with the knowledge and resources needed to thrive in the world of cut flower farming. From educational workshops and conferences to online resources and publications, they provide a wealth of information and support for all things related to growing exceptional cut ...

title: RCSI MyHealth Series - Sleep through the lifecycle: a toolkit for resting deeply at every stage in life
summary: he final episode of the RCSI MyHealth series 2024/25 explores the crucial role of sleep in our overall health, wellbeing and performance.В  Despite its importance, many people struggle to achieve sufficient and restorative sleep. In this episode, we will delve into what happens to the brain and body during sleep, the key factors that influence sleep quality, and evidence-based tools to improve it. We will also examine the impact of sleep on memory, learning, and cognitive function, as well as its deep connections to mental health. This episode is chaired byВ Dr Jolanta Burke,В Associate Professor, RCSI Centre for Positive Health SciencesВ Panellists include:    Professor Richard Costello,В Professor of Respiratory Medicine, Department of Medicine RCSI & Consultant in Respiratory and Sleep Medicine, Beaumont HospitalВ  В     Dr Maria Pertl, Lecturer, Department of Health Psychology, School of Population Health, RCSI; Chartered Health Psychologist; Certified Lifestyle Medicine practitioner, British Society of Lifestyle Medicine, and member of the Irish Society of Lifestyle Medicine В     Mr Motty Varghese,В Sleep Physiologist, The Sleep Therapy Clinic

summary: After the marathon, I gave myself more space to recover than ever before - and I still found myself overriding what my body was asking for. title: Back Yourself to Win: Why Mindset Determines Your Fitness Results
summary: Ever wonder why two people can follow the exact same fitness program and get completely different results? In this powerful episode, Mandy & Olly dive into the surprising truth: itвҖҷs not the plan, itвҖҷs the person. YouвҖҷll see how our most successful clients approach the same workouts, nutrition, and challengesвҖ”yet succeed where others struggle. This isnвҖҷt about genetics or willpower. ItвҖҷs about mindset, belief, and commitment. Whether you're just starting your journey or have been stuck in the вҖңon-again, off-againвҖқ loop, this video will help you understand what actually makes the difference between real progress and constant frustration. рҹ’Ў What youвҖҷll learn in this episode: вң… Why mindsetвҖ”not methodвҖ”is the #1 driver of results вң… The key differences between our most and least successful clients вң… How to spot a self-sabotaging mindset before it ruins your progress вң… Simple strategies to build self-trust and consistency вң… How to stay on track when motivation fades This isnвҖҷt motivational fluffвҖ”itвҖҷs a roadmap to long-term results based on real client outcomes and behavior patterns weвҖҷve seen over and over again. рҹ‘ү Whether your goal is fat loss, strength, better habits, or more energy, backing yourself is the first real step. рҹҺҜ Need help building a plan that works with your life, not against it? title: Ep 398: Inside Biwi with Cait Bazemore
summary: This week on the podcast, Worn & Wound contributor Cait Bazemore makes her podcast debut. Cait has been writing about watches for years, and in this episode we spend most of our time discussing her recent piece on Biwi, a Swiss firm which has gained some fame recently for their work with Norqain. Biwi is a fascinating company and has manufactured rubber straps and a whole host of other things for watch brands over the years, and the story of how Cait came across the story and what she discovered at BiwiвҖҷs facilities in Switzerland is truly fascinating. Before the Biwi talk begins, Cait takes us through her path in the watch industry, whatвҖҷs interesting to her about the community, and how her interest in traditional craftsmanship relates to the high tech world of Biwi. В  To stay on top of all new episodes, you can subscribe to The Worn & Wound Podcast on all major platforms including Apple Podcasts, Stitcher, Spotify, and more. You can also find our RSS feed here. And if you like what you hear, then donвҖҷt forget to leave us a review. If thereвҖҷs a question you want us to answer you can hit us up at info@wornandwound.com, and weвҖҷll put your question in the queue. WeвҖҷre covering: How to know if youвҖҷre eating the right amount of protein, what it means if youвҖҷre always hungry, if itвҖҷs okay to go over your fats or carbs, tips for beginners who have never tracked before, how to build muscle and lose fat (and if itвҖҷs even possible at the same time), reverse dieting, intuitive eating, restaurant tracking hacks and more! summary: иҝҷдёҖжңҹжҳҜжҲ‘д»¬жҫіжҙІжҲҝдә§зі»еҲ—зҡ„з¬¬дёҖжңҹпјҢжқҘиҒҠиҒҠдҪңдёәжҲҝдёңз®ЎзҗҶStrataзҡ„йӮЈдәӣзіҹеҝғдәӢгҖӮ жҲ‘д»¬зҡ„еҳүе®ҫйҳҝй»„жҳҜеңЁе°Ҹзәўд№Ұи®ӨиҜҶзҡ„гҖӮд»–зҡ„иҙҰеҸ·вҖңйҳҝй»„еңЁжӮүе°јвҖқдё“й—Ёи®ІStrataзҡ„е№Іиҙ§пјҢиҝҳжңүдёӘ200еӨҡдәәзҡ„дәӨжөҒдә’еҠ©зҫӨпјҢжҲҝдёңд»¬дёҖиө·и®Ёи®әеҲҶдә«Strataзҡ„зҹҘиҜҶгҖӮ жҲ‘иҮӘе·ұд№ҹеӯҰдәҶдёҚе°‘дёңиҘҝгҖӮ е…ҲеЈ°жҳҺдёҖдёӢпјҢжҲ‘д»¬д»ҠеӨ©иҜҙзҡ„еҶ…е®№еҸӘжҳҜдёӘдәәи§ӮзӮ№е’ҢдәІиә«з»ҸеҺҶпјҢд»…дҫӣеҸӮиҖғпјҢе№¶дёҚжҳҜдё“дёҡж„Ҹи§ҒгҖӮе…·дҪ“й—®йўҳиҜ·е’ЁиҜўдё“дёҡдәәеЈ«гҖӮ Timestamps 00:41 жӮүе°јдёҡдё»йҳҝй»„еҲҶдә«Strataз®ЎзҗҶзҡ„з§Қз§Қй—®йўҳ 05:06дёҡдё»з»ҙжқғеӣ°еўғпјҡжӣҙжҚўзү©дёҡе…¬еҸёжҲҗе”ҜдёҖеҮәи·ҜгҖӮ 08:55жӣҙжҚўзү©дёҡе…¬еҸёзҡ„иҝҮзЁӢ 10:31 зү©дёҡз®ЎзҗҶе…¬еҸёйҖүжӢ©ж ҮеҮҶ 17:56 зү©дёҡз®ЎзҗҶеҗҲеҗҢзңӢд»Җд№Ҳ-иҙ№з”Ёи°ҲеҲӨзҡ„е®һжҲҳз»ҸйӘҢ 29:25 жҚўдәҶзү©дёҡз®ЎзҗҶе…¬еҸёзҡ„жҲҗж•Ҳ 31:05 зү©дёҡз®ЎзҗҶдёӯйңҖиҰҒжіЁж„Ҹзҡ„еқ‘ 42:41 зү©дёҡз®ЎзҗҶзҡ„tips 44:03 зү©дёҡз®ЎзҗҶзҡ„й«ҳжҲҗжң¬дёҺеӨҚжқӮжҖ§ 45:33 еҚҺдәәеҸӮдёҺStrataз®ЎзҗҶзҡ„зҺ°зҠ¶дёҺжҢ‘жҲҳ 47:03 й«ҳд»·жңҚеҠЎзҡ„зңҹзӣёпјҡдёәдҪ•жңүдәӣе…¬еҸёж”¶иҙ№й«ҳеҚҙжӣҙйҖҸжҳҺ рҹ’¬ дә’еҠЁпјҡеҰӮжһңдҪ жңүиў«Strataеқ‘иҝҮжҲ–иҖ…дҪңдёәStrataд»ҺдёҡиҖ…жңүдёҚеҗҢзңӢжі•пјҢж¬ўиҝҺз•ҷиЁҖпјҢеҸҜиғҪиў«йҖүдёәдёӢжңҹйҖүйўҳпјҒ йҳҝй»„еңЁе°Ҹзәўд№Ұжңү200еӨҡдәәзҡ„жҫіжҙІStrataдәӨжөҒзҫӨпјҢжҸҗдҫӣдә’еҠ©з»ҸйӘҢпјҢжңүе…ҙи¶Јзҡ„еҗ¬дј—еҸҜд»ҘеңЁе°Ҹзәўд№ҰеҠ рҹ‘ү @йҳҝй»„еңЁжӮүе°јгҖӮ гҖҗдә’еҠЁж–№ејҸгҖ‘ е°Ҹзәўд№Ұпјҡ йҘӯйҘӯиҖҢи°ҲSydney Emailпјҡricetalksydney@outlook.com

title: Tending Whats In Your Reach
summary: Hey there darlings, welcome back to the Roots and Refuge Podcast. TodayвҖҷs episode is one I recorded as much for myself as for you. ItвҖҷs a message I wish 22-year-old me could have heardвҖ”back when myВ dreams felt so big and my limits felt so loud.  If youвҖҷve ever felt overwhelmed by what you canвҖҷt do, this episode is a gentle reminder that thereвҖҷs sacred strength in doing what you can. IвҖҷm talking about tending your life like a gardenвҖ”not in panic, not in perfection, but in presence. Whether you're in a season of limitations, raising babies, or just navigating a world that feels a bit too urgent, youвҖҷre not alone. There is wisdom in your limits. There is grace in your pace. And there is peace in choosing to be rooted right where you are.  This conversation touches on preparation, presence, performance, and the quiet rebellion of slow living. Let it encourage you to stop striving, to tend with care, and to trust that whatвҖҷs within your reach is enough.  If youвҖҷve found encouragement here, consider supporting the podcast on Patreon, where you can listen early and join our monthly Q&A.  As always, I bless you until next time.

title: How to Choose the Right Supplements (and Avoid the Wrong Ones)
summary: With so many supplements on the market, how do you know whatвҖҷs actually worth takingвҖ”and whatвҖҷs just a waste of money? In this episode of The Dr. Hyman Show, I sit down with Steve Martocci, a tech entrepreneur whoвҖҷs applying smart tools and real data to help people finally make sense of their supplements. We talk about why most people still arenвҖҷt getting the nutrients they needвҖ”even if theyвҖҷre taking vitaminsвҖ”and how SteveвҖҷs new platform, SuppCo, is making it easier to figure out what to take, what to skip, and what to trust. YouвҖҷll learn: вҖў Why 70% of people are still low in at least one important nutrient (and how that affects your energy, mood, sleep, and more) вҖў The truth about getting everything you need from food (and why thatвҖҷs harder than it sounds) вҖў How to spot low-quality supplements and avoid harmful fillers and contaminants вҖў Why your doctor may not know how supplements interact with your medications вҖў How to SuppCoвҖҷs app can help you track all your supplements, avoid taking too much of certain nutrients, and personalize your health routine  (BTW, when youВ download the SuppCo app, you can view my personal supplement Stack and Protocols). If you take supplementsвҖ”or are thinking about itвҖ”this episode will help you do it smarter, safer, and more effectively. title: Veggie Gardens, Food Security & the Real Cost of Growing Your Own Food
summary: Another sit down with Regan & Kirsty as we dive into all things 'homestead gardening & food security' - a topic that's becoming more and more relevant, even outside of homesteading, as the world's systems of food, health and personal freedoms feel less and less reliable every day.We're only one year into our Homesteading journey, but we've brought nearly 15 years of gardening experience into it. We knew growing our own food was going to be a key ingredient to what we built and what we teach, but we probably didn't realise just how important this message would become. As families and communities suffer under a burdening cost of living crisis and the rising prices of food and nutrition wreack havoc on everyday budgets, we want to share some genuine insights into our own food security plans and just how realistic they are. title: 2025 Kia K4
summary: We purchased a 2025 Kia K4 LXS for CR's test program. At $24,320, the K4 is priced surprisingly low for a new car. Does it deliver value for money? title: How Can I Build Stronger Bones?
summary: This week on Health Matters, Courtney Allison is joined by Dr. Erica Eldon, physiatrist with NewYork-Presbyterian and Weill Cornell Medicine. They discuss how our body builds strong and healthy bones, and what happens to our bones as they ageвҖ”including why they can get brittle and contribute to things like breaks in hips or wrists, and even losing height in our spine. Dr. Eldon describes the key factors in maintaining bone health over time. She explains why itвҖҷs especially important for women to incorporate resistance training into their self care, and offers some practical steps for everyone to take toward strong, healthy bones at every phase of life. ___ Dr. Erica Eldon, DO, is doctor of interventional spine care with NewYork-Presbyterian and Columbia. She specializes in treating patients with acute and chronic musculoskeletal and spine injuries. Dr. Eldon is board eligible in both Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation and Pain Medicine. She received her Doctorate of Osteopathic Medicine (DO) from Midwestern University-Arizona College of Osteopathic Medicine, underwent residency training in Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation at the Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai Hospital where she also served as Chief Resident, and completed a multidisciplinary pain medicine fellowship at Weill Cornell/NewYork Presbyterian Hospital, Hospital for Special Surgery, and Memorial Sloan Kettering Hospital. ___ Health Matters is your weekly dose of health and wellness information, from the leading experts. Join host Courtney Allison to get news you can use in your own life. New episodes drop each Wednesday. If you are looking for practical health tips and trustworthy information from world-class doctors and medical experts you will enjoy listening to Health Matters. Health Matters was created to share stories of science, care, and wellness that are happening every day at NewYork-Presbyterian, one of the nationвҖҷs most comprehensive, integrated academic healthcare systems. In keeping with NewYork-PresbyterianвҖҷs long legacy of medical breakthroughs and innovation, Health Matters features the latest news, insights, and health tips from our trusted experts; inspiring first-hand accounts from patients and caregivers; and updates on the latest research and innovations in patient care, all in collaboration with our renowned medical schools, Columbia and Weill Cornell Medicine. To learn more visit: https://healthmatters.nyp.org

title: Rachel Holmes Podcast show with Special Guest Rachel Hubbard
summary: Exciting NewsвҖҰ The Rachel Holmes Podcast is BACK!  ItвҖҷs hard to believe, but this podcast has been going for 8 years (on and off!). Like I always sayвҖ”itвҖҷs all about consistencyвҖҰ and IвҖҷm finally taking my own advice!  Podcasts are a big part of my lifeвҖ”they get me through long car journeys, tedious house chores, cleaning, folding laundryвҖҰ IвҖҷve always got one playing! And of course, it seems like everyone has a podcast these days. But today is a special oneвҖҰ IвҖҷm finally recording an episode IвҖҷve wanted to do for over 6 months, and I couldnвҖҷt be more excited! IвҖҷll be chatting with the incredible Rachel HubbardвҖ”  Exercise & Menopause Specialist  Pilates Coach & Back Care Tutor  CIMSPA Training Provider & YMCA Technical Advisor  Motivational Speaker & Leading Expert in WomenвҖҷs Health Rachel also co-wrote the brand-new Level 3 Menopause Practitioner Course that we proudly deliver, and I canвҖҷt wait to dive into all things menopause, fitness, and changing perceptions around womenвҖҷs health! Check it out like and subscribe!

summary: WeвҖҷre excited to introduce you to Confessions of a Female Founder with Meghan, a new show from Lemonada Media.  В   As Meghan, Duchess of Sussex builds out a business of her own, sheвҖҷs getting advice and insights from a handful of amazing women who have scaled small ideas into successful companies. summary: In this weekвҖҷs episode, Dancing With The Stars pro Val Chmerkovskiy returns (two years after he and Jenna Johnson were among our very first guests!). Val opens up about growing up in Ukraine until age eight, immigrating to Brooklyn, and what that huge life shift felt like as a kid. He shares when dance first entered his life, how it evolved from a creative outlet to a career, and what itвҖҷs like to keep that passion alive when itвҖҷs also your job. He talks about working alongside his wife Jenna as pros on Dancing With The Stars, their shared Emmy nomination for a tribute to Len Goodman, and how they navigate turning off вҖңwork modeвҖқ at home. Val also speaks about how fatherhood has shifted his perspective and deepened his appreciation for his own parentsвҖҷ sacrifices. Plus, he gives us the inside scoop on filming HuluвҖҷs Got to Get Out, how the experience challenged him, and the friendships he formed with his fellow castmates. title: S3 E8 - Squashes and Courgettes
podcast_name: The Potty Plotters Plotcast - Growing on the Allotment
summary: In this lively episode of the Potty Plotters Plotcast, Julia and Elaine return to discuss the delights of growing pumpkins, squashes, and sweet corn, as well as the intricacies of succession planting. The episode kicks off with a humorous exchange about the recent warm weather and its effects on their allotment activities, including a comical mishap involving sunstroke and mistaken identities. As they delve into the main topics, the duo shares their tips and tricks for successfully cultivating pumpkins and squashes, discussing the differences between the two and the ideal conditions for growth. They also touch upon the importance of proper watering techniques and the benefits of using compost to retain moisture. Listeners will learn about the challenges of growing courgettes, including disease resistance and the need for adequate spacing to ensure healthy plants. Julia and Elaine emphasise the significance of succession planting, encouraging gardeners to continuously sow crops like sweet peas and radishes to extend their harvests. The episode wraps up with a light-hearted discussion about the joys of sweet corn, including the best planting practices and the potential challenges posed by pests. With plenty of laughter, personal anecdotes, and practical advice, this episode is a must-listen for anyone looking to enhance their gardening skills. title: The Metrics: Blood Sugar Basics!
summary: In this gentle and empowering episode, CatherineвҖ”host of The Diabetic MammyвҖ”welcomes both new and seasoned parents of children with type 1 diabetes. Titled вҖңThe Metrics,вҖқ this episode is inspired by two mothers who, despite their different points on the diabetes journey, both revealed they had never been clearly taught what key diabetes numbers mean. This episode walks listeners through the essential metrics of diabetes management with warmth and clarity: Glycemic Control вҖ“ the overall aim of managing blood sugars in a safe range, with emphasis on balance over perfection.Blood Glucose Levels (BGL) вҖ“ explained in both mmol/L and mg/dL, with realistic goals tailored to each child.HbA1c вҖ“ a long-term measure of average blood sugars, usually targeted under 7%, but personalized.Time in Range (TIR) вҖ“ a newer CGM-based concept reflecting how often blood sugars stay in the target range. Catherine reassures listeners that understanding these numbers is a process and that it's okay not to have it all figured out at once. She emphasizes that good glycemic control supports physical health, energy, and emotional well-being, but also reminds parents that numbers arenвҖҷt everythingвҖ”their childвҖҷs joy, safety, and spirit matter just as much. She closes with an uplifting message of community, validation, and encouragement: You are doing a great job. YouвҖҷre not alone. And this isnвҖҷt a raceвҖ”itвҖҷs a relationship. The Diabetic Mammy is the heartfelt, no-nonsense podcast where type 1 diabetes meets motherhood, community, and a whole lot of real talk. Hosted by Catherine, a mammy navigating the highs and lows of type 1 diabetes, each episode blends personal insight with practical wisdom, offering support for parents, carers, and anyone living with or loving someone with diabetes. From tech transitions and hospital diagnoses to school lunches and stolen sleep, this podcast embraces the messy, meaningful moments that make up life with chronic illness. Whether youвҖҷre newly diagnosed or a seasoned warrior, The Diabetic Mammy is your space to feel seen, supported, and never alone.
